title: The Grove Neighborhood, St. Louis
content:
The Grove is the entertainment district within Forest Park Southeast, along Manchester Avenue between Kingshighway and Vandeventer. Known for diverse dining, nightlife, arts, and LGBTQ-friendly environment.

HISTORY: Originally The Manchester Strip serving working-class population. Declined by 1950s, faced urban renewal threats. LGBTQ+ community investment sparked revitalization. Community Improvement District established 2009.

HOUSING:
- Historic: working-class homes from 1890s, 800-1,500 sq ft, $125,000-$600,000
- New developments: Adam's Grove (50 units), Union at The Grove (student housing), Chroma (235 apts), Terra at The Grove (307 apts)
- National Historic District (more architectural freedom than Lafayette Square or Benton Park)

DINING & NIGHTLIFE:
- Urban Chestnut Grove Brewery (German-style, Wolpertinger festival)
- Grace Meat + Three (Southern cuisine, fried chicken)
- La Calle STL (authentic Mexican), Sanctuaria (international tapas)
- Serendipity Ice Cream (boozy + traditional), The Gramophone (sandwiches)
- Handlebar (two-story dance venue), Hot Java (800-person concert venue)
- Just John (LGBTQ club), Prism (drag shows, karaoke)
- Platypus (mocktails), Taha'a Twisted Tiki

SHOPPING: Intoxicology (barware, bartending classes), City Greens Market, Chakra Wellness, Crimson Serpents Outpost

NEARBY: Central West End, Tower Grove, Missouri Botanical Gardens, Delmar Loop, Forest Park (Science Center, The Muny, Zoo)

EDUCATION: Metro High School (#1 magnet in MO, #9 best public HS). Nearby: Wash U, SLU, St. Louis University High School.
